functions.definition: camelCase(input: string, settings?: CamelCaseSettings): string
functions.comment: Transform a string into a camelCased word (eg. 'camel case' -> 'camelCase')
examples:
camelCase('data-value2-input'); // --> dataValue2input
camelCase('XML data input'); // --> XmlDataInput

// With settings
const settings = { abbr: true, numbers: true, upper: true };

camelCase('data-VALUE2-input', settings); // --> DataVALUE2Input
functions.descriptions: @returns : The formatted string

functions.definition: capitalize(str: string): string
functions.comment: Capitalize each word in a phrase
examples:
capitalize('capitalize this phrase'); // --> Capitalize This Phrase
capitalize('capitalize-This-phrase'); // --> Capitalize-This-Phrase
functions.descriptions: @returns : Capitalized string

functions.definition: chunkString(str: string, options?: ChunkStringOptions): string[]
functions.comment: Split a String up into smaller strings of a give size (eg. 'ABCDEF' -> [AB,CD,EF])
examples:
chunkString('abcdefghijkl'); // --> ['ab', 'cd', 'ef', 'gh', 'ij', 'kl']
chunkString('abcdefghij', { size: 4 }); // --> ['abcd', 'efgh', 'ij']
chunkString('abcdefghij', { size: 4, reverse: true }); // --> ['ab', 'cdef', 'ghij']
functions.descriptions: @returns : Array of chunks

functions.definition: debounce<Textends DebounceCallback>(callback: T, ms?: number): DebouncedFunction<T>
functions.comment:
Ensure that the givcen callback function will only be called ms milliseconds after the last call.
This ensures to not trigger too many consecutive calls, but only the last in the sequence.
functions.descriptions: @returns : The debounce enabled function

functions.definition: formatCurrency(thousandTemplate?: string): CurrencyFormatter
functions.comment:
Creates a function that formats a number to a given currency format (eg. 1000 -> 1.000,00 €)

The template string should be the number 1000 described with before and after
symbols (no numbers), a thousand separator and a decimal separator followed by
the number of decimals defined with zeroes: [before]1[thou.]000[dec.]00[after] -> $ 1,000.00
examples:
// Format number to default currency format (euro)
const euro = formatCurrency();
euro(2345234.678); // --> '2.345.234,68 €'

// Format number to USD currency format
const usd = formatCurrency('$ 1,000.00');
usd(2345234.678); // --> '$ 2,345,234.68'

// Format number to custom currency format
const custom = formatCurrency('# 1-000;00 ¤');
custom(2345234.678); // --> '# 2-345-234;68 ¤'

// Specifying number of decimals
const sixDecimals = formatCurrency('$ 1,000.000000');
sixDecimals(2345234.678); // --> '$ 2,345,234.678000'
sixDecimals(234.12345678); // --> '$ 234.123457'
functions.descriptions: @returns : Curried function to format a given number

functions.definition: formatNumber(num: number, settings?: FormatNumberSettings): string
functions.comment:
Formats a number with defined thousand and decimal separator, and a decimal limit
(see limitDecimals for details on decimalCount)
examples:
// Default format
formatNumber(123456); // --> 123.456,00

// Custom format
formatNumber(123456, { decimalCount: '>3', thousand: '-', decimal: ':' }); // --> 123-456:000
functions.descriptions: @returns : Formatted number as a string

functions.definition: fuzzySearch(input: string, searchWord: string, options?: FuzzySearchOptions): boolean
functions.comment: Match the given search word (collection of characters) could be found in in a given input (word or phrase)
examples:
fuzzySearch('search me', 'src') // => true
fuzzySearch('search me', 'ache') // => true
fuzzySearch('search me', 'reach') // => false
functions.descriptions: @returns : if the searchWord was found in the input or not

functions.definition: hash(str: string): string
functions.comment: Generates a unique hash (DJB2) string from a string
examples: hash('Hash this string'); // --> sg463l
functions.descriptions: @returns : Hash string

functions.definition: hashCode(str: string): number
functions.comment: Generates a unique numeric hash (DJB2) code from a string
examples: hashCode('Hash this string'); // --> 1720121313
functions.descriptions: @returns : Hash code

functions.definition: hexToNumber(hex: string): number
functions.descriptions: @returns : Numeric representation of the hex code

functions.definition: hexToRGB(hex: string): number[]
functions.comment: Converts a Hexadecimal color to a RGB(A) color array
examples:
hexToRGB('#fff'); // --> [255, 255, 255]
hexToRGB('#2fd466'); // --> [47, 212, 102]

// And with alpha channel
hexToRGB('#2fd46680'); // --> [47, 212, 102, 0.5]
functions.descriptions: @returns : Array with RGB values

functions.definition: isBoolean(x: unknown): boolean (x is boolean)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument a Boolean or not

functions.definition: isFunction(x: unknown): boolean (x is (args: unknown[]) => unknown)
functions.comment: Is the given argument a Function
examples:
isFunction(() => {}); // --> true
isFunction('string'); // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument a Function or not

functions.definition: isGenerator(x: unknown): boolean (x is GeneratorFunction)
functions.comment: Determine if the given argument is a Generator Function
examples:
function* gen() {}

isGenerator(gen); // --> true
isGenerator(() => {}); // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument a Generator or not

functions.definition: isGeneratorLike(x: unknown): boolean (x is Generator<unknown, any, any>)
functions.comment:
Determine if the given argument is a Generator object.
(A generator is the one created when calling a generator function)
examples:
function *gen() {}

isGeneratorLike(gen()); // --> true
isGeneratorLike({ next() {}, throw() {} return() {} [Symbol.iterator]() {} }); // --> true
isGeneratorLike(() => {}); // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument a Generator like function or not

functions.definition: isNumber(x: unknown): boolean (x is number)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument a finite number or not

functions.definition: isNumeric(x: unknown): boolean
functions.comment: Is the given argument is a finite numeric value (number as string or number directly) or not.
examples:
isNumeric(123); // --> true
isNumeric('123'); // --> true
isNumeric(Infinity); // --> false
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the given argument is a numeric value

functions.definition: isObject(x: unknown): boolean (x is object)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument a plain object or not

functions.definition: isString(x: unknown): boolean (x is string)
functions.descriptions: @returns : Whether the argument is a string or not

functions.definition: kebabCase(str: string, settings?: PhrasifySettings): string
functions.comment:
Transform phrase into a dashed phrase
(eg. 'camelCase' -> 'camel-case' or 'spaced phrase' -> 'spaced-phrase')
examples:
kebabCase('some dashed phrase'); // --> some-dashed-phrase
kebabCase('dash version1 beta', { numbers: true }); // --> dash-version-1-beta
functions.descriptions: @returns : The string with spaces replaced by a dash (-)

functions.definition: leadingZero(num: number | string, len?: number): string
functions.descriptions: @returns : The given number as a string padded with zeroes to match the given min length

functions.definition: limitDecimals(num: number, decimalCount?: string | number): string
functions.comment:
Limit decimals of a floating number to specified length. The length depends on decimalCount
which can have the following settings (eg. 2 or ">3" or "<5"):

Char Description
>n Minimum number of decimals, if the current number of decimals are shorter than the defined length, extra 0 (zeros) will be added.
<n Maximum number of decimals, longer decimals will be rounded and shortened down to this number.
n Match this exact number of decimals, rounding longer decimals and adding extra 0 (zeroes) to shorter ones.
examples:
// Exact number of decimals
limitDecimals(123.4567) // --> 123.46
limitDecimals(123, 5) // --> 123.00000

// Max number of decimals
limitDecimals(123.4567, '<3') // --> 123.457
limitDecimals(123, '<3') // --> 123

// Min number decimals
limitDecimals(123.4, '>4') // --> 123.4000
limitDecimals(123.456789, '>4') // --> 123.456789

// Min, Max number decimals
limitDecimals(123.4, '2,4') // --> 123.40
limitDecimals(123.456789, '2,4') // --> 123.4568
functions.descriptions: @returns : String representation of the number with the decimals adjusted according to the decimal setting

functions.definition: minMax(num: number, min: number, max: number): number
functions.descriptions: @returns : The number limited to the min/max boundaries

functions.definition: numberToHex(num: number): string
functions.descriptions: @returns : Hex representation of the given number

functions.definition: pascalCase(input: string, settings?: PascalCaseSettings): string
functions.descriptions: @returns : The formatted string

functions.definition: phrasify(input: string, settings?: PhrasifySettings): string
functions.comment: Transform a string into a space separated phrase
examples:
phrasify('XMLDataInput'); // --> XML data input
phrasify('dataVALUE2-input', { numbers: true }); // --> data VALUE 2 input
functions.descriptions: @returns : The transformed phrase or word

functions.definition: popAtIndex<T>(list: T[], index: number): T
functions.comment: Removes and returns an entry from a given array, at a designated index position.
examples: popIndex([1,2,3], 1); // --> 2 (array will then be [1, 3])
remarks: This is not a pure function and will alter the given array internally

functions.definition: popAtIndexPure(list: unknown[], index: number): [unknown, unknown[]]
functions.comment: Removes and returns an entry from a given array, at a designated index position.
examples: popIndexPure([1,2,3], 1); // --> [2,  [1, 3]]
functions.descriptions:
@returns : An array with two entries: the first entry is the value just removed and the second
is the new array with the entry removed.

functions.definition: promisefy(fn: PromisefyCallback): PromisefiedFunction
functions.comment: Converts a callback based action into one returning a Promise instead.
examples:
function action(name, callback) { ... callback(); }

action = promisefy(action);

action
  .then(() => 'all good')
  .catch(() => 'Something went wrong');
functions.descriptions: @returns : The promisefied function

functions.definition: randomCryptoId(length?: number): string
functions.comment:
Generate a random id of designated length - using cryptographic secure random numbers,
so it is a bit slower than randomId
examples:
randomCryptoId(); // --> eg. 'efuc6f1n4xf'
randomCryptoId(20); // --> eg. '3vsmrbxlh9at0vhcsf1xh'
remarks:
This method differs from crypto.randomUUID() in the way that it can be of any specified length
and doesn't contain any "-".
The methods would be slightly slower than randomId since it is using crypto.getRandomValues(),
but it is a more secure method.
functions.descriptions: @returns : A random generated id of a specified length

functions.definition: randomHexColor(): string
functions.descriptions: @returns : A random hex color

functions.definition: randomId(length?: number): string
functions.comment: Generate a random id of designated length
examples:
randomId(); // --> eg. 'efuc6f1n4xf'
randomId(20); // --> eg. '3vsmrbxlh9at0vhcsf1xh'
functions.descriptions: @returns : A random generated id of a specified length

functions.definition: randomInt(min: number, max: number): number
functions.comment: Returns a random integer from a base number or range of numbers
examples: randomInt(100, 200); // --> a number between 100 and 200
functions.descriptions: @returns : A random number between the given min and max

functions.definition: randomInt(max?: number): number
functions.comment: Returns a random integer from a base number or range of numbers
examples:
// Any random number
randomInt(); // --> a number between 0 and Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER

// With max number
randomInt(100); // --> a number between 0 and 100
functions.descriptions: @returns : A random number between 0 and the given max

functions.definition: randomRGBColor(): [r: number, g: number, b: number]
functions.descriptions: @returns : An Array with random R G B values

functions.definition: RGBToHex(rgb: RGBTuple): string
functions.comment: Converts an Array of R G B (A) colors into a hex color.
examples:
RGBToHex([123, 123, 123]) // --> #7b7b7b

// With alpha channel
RGBToHex([123, 123, 123, 0.5]) // --> #7b7b7b80
functions.descriptions: @returns : A Hex representation of the given color

functions.definition: RGBToHex(r: number, g: number, b: number, a?: number): string
functions.comment: Converts R G B (A) color arguments into a hex color.
examples:
// RGB as arguments
RGBToHex( 123, 123, 123 ) // --> #7b7b7b80

// With alpha channel
RGBToHex( 123, 123, 123, 0.5 ) // --> #7b7b7b80
functions.descriptions: @returns : A Hex representation of the given colors

functions.definition: safeDateChange(from: Date, to: Date): Date
functions.comment:
Verifies and corrects Dates where the month could accidentally have skipped into the
next month because the date is out of bounds by the month changed to.
examples:
const date = new Date(2017, 0, 31);
newDate = new Date(date);
newDate.setMonth(1);

// Normally you would expect "newDate" month to be February, but since the date
// of the previous date was 31 and february max date is 28 (or 29), the actual
// "newDate" is "Match 3rd 2017" (or 2nd). Using this function keeps it at "February 28 2017"

safeDateChange(date, newDate);

// newDate === "February 28 2017"
functions.descriptions: @returns : Altered "to" date

functions.definition: snakeCase(str: string, settings?: PhrasifySettings): string
functions.comment:
Transform phrase into a snake_case phrase
(eg. 'camelCase' -> 'camel-case' or 'spaced phrase' -> 'spaced-phrase')
examples:
snakeCase('Convert This phrase'); // --> convert_this_phrase
snakeCase('dash VERSION1 beta', { numbers: true }); // --> dash_version_1_beta
functions.descriptions: @returns : The string transformed to snake_case

functions.definition: throttle<Textends (args: unknown[]) => void>(callback: T, ms?: number): T
functions.comment: Ensure that the given callback function can only be called every ms milliseconds
functions.descriptions: @returns : The throttled function

functions.definition: truncate(str: string, settings?: TruncateSettings): string
functions.comment: Limits a string to a given number of characters and adds '...' in the end
examples:
truncate('No max length to the string'); // --> No max limit to the string length
truncate('With a max length to the string', { maxLength: 10 }); // --> With a max...
truncate('With a max length to the string and a different ending', { maxLength: 10, end: ' <---' }); // --> With a max <---
functions.descriptions: @returns : The truncated string

functions.definition: uniqueArray<T>(arr: ArrayLike<T> | Iterable<T, any, any>): T[]
functions.comment: Filter out duplicate values from an array
examples:
uniqueArray([1,2,3,1,4,5,3,7]); // --> [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
uniqueArray(document.querySelectorAll('.my-elm, .selected')); // --> unique array of elements (so .my-elm.selected will only be present once)
functions.descriptions: @returns : A list with only unique items

customTypes.name: CamelCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown:
type CamelCaseSettings = PhrasifySettings & {
  // Keep abbreviations uppercase (false == HTMLElement => HtmlElement | true == HTMLElement => HTMLElement)
  abbr?: boolean
  // Convert to UpperCase CamelCase (aka PascalCase)
  upper?: boolean
};

customTypes.name: ChunkStringOptions
customTypes.markdown:
type ChunkStringOptions = {
  // Chunk strings from the end to front (['ab', 'cde', 'fgh'] instead of ['abc', 'def', 'gh'])
  reverse?: boolean
  // The max size of each chunk
  size?: number
};

customTypes.name: DebounceCallback
customTypes.markdown: type DebounceCallback = (args: unknown[]) => void;

customTypes.name: DebouncedFunction
customTypes.markdown:
type DebouncedFunction = T & {
  // Method to cancel the current debounce
  cancelDebounce: () => void
};

customTypes.name: CurrencyFormatter
customTypes.markdown: type CurrencyFormatter = (num: number) => string;

customTypes.name: FormatNumberSettings
customTypes.markdown:
type FormatNumberSettings = {
  // The decimal separator character
  decimal?: string
  // How many decimals to show
  decimalCount?: number | string
  // The thousand separator character
  thousand?: string
};

customTypes.name: FuzzySearchOptions
customTypes.markdown:
type FuzzySearchOptions = {
  // Respect the case of the input, or not
  caseSensitive: boolean
};

customTypes.name: KebabCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown: type KebabCaseSettings = PhrasifySettings;

customTypes.name: PascalCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown: type PascalCaseSettings = Omit<CamelCaseSettings, 'upper'>;

customTypes.name: PhrasifySettings
customTypes.markdown:
type PhrasifySettings = {
  // Make numbers the delimeter of a word or not
  numbers?: boolean
};

customTypes.name: PromisefiedFunction
customTypes.markdown: type PromisefiedFunction = (args: any[]) => Promise<any>;

customTypes.name: PromisefyCallback
customTypes.markdown: type PromisefyCallback = (args: any[]) => any;

customTypes.name: RGBTuple
customTypes.markdown: type RGBTuple = [red: number, green: number, blue: number, alpha?: number];

customTypes.name: SnakeCaseSettings
customTypes.markdown: type SnakeCaseSettings = PhrasifySettings;

customTypes.name: TruncateSettings
customTypes.markdown:
type TruncateSettings = {
  // What ending to give the truncated string (default is "...")
  end?: string
  // Max length of the given string
  maxLength?: number
};
